case_study_exempt_reason: Adversarial backend test-coverage (TC-T9): adds chaos/edge tests to existing test files. No product surface; PR body + this state.json are the provenance record.
summary: T9 (test-coverage plan §4) FOUNDATION — adversarial backend chaos/edge tests. First slice: 3 EncryptionService chaos tests on the session-context path (concurrent round-trips / actor serialization, ~2MB large-payload round-trip, 50-payload no-cross-contamination). NOTE: the biometric-gated rotateKeys path is untestable on the CI simulator (LAError -7 no enrolled authenticator) — deferred to T2 pending biometry-enrolled CI or a prod test seam. Remaining bullet areas (sync reconciliation, GDPR cascade, network-churn auth) are T3 follow-ups. Test-only, no prod change.

tasks.title: EncryptionService session-context chaos tests (3): concurrency, large-payload, no-cross-contamination
Key-rotation chaos tests (rotateKeys) — needs biometry-enrolled CI simulator or a prod test seam for authenticatedContext()
GDPR cascade (CloudKit+Supabase) + network-churn auth recovery chaos tests
tasks.notes: Shipped 6 seam-free, isolation-safe chaos tests in AccountDeletionServiceTests.swift: network-churn resilience (request/cancel survive best-effort remote-sync failure — local is source of truth per Audit BE-023), rapid request/cancel alternation state-machine consistency, checkGracePeriod relaunch idempotency, and a 30-day grace-boundary sweep (daysRemaining clamp + monotonicity + whole-day-truncation expiry semantics). 12/12 AccountDeletionServiceTests pass on iPhone 17 sim (Xcode 26.6). The destructive full executeDeletion() partial-failure cascade is split to T4 (needs a sync-service seam).

tasks.title: Destructive full executeDeletion() partial-failure cascade test — requires an injectable protocol seam over SupabaseSyncService/CloudKitSyncService (both `final` + high-risk-area)
tasks.notes: Same blocker class as T2 (needs a prod test seam). The final sync services can't be fault-injected without a protocol abstraction, and that change lands in high-risk files (SupabaseSyncService.swift / CloudKitSyncService.swift) requiring the extra-review policy — deferred to a reviewed seam PR rather than forced with fragile, non-isolated real-network tests.
